doc: Multichannel-out demo: three bound output channels — the playable 8-voice synth on stereo mains (logical channels 0/1) plus a dedicated low-passed sub send on logical channel 2. Each output pipe is one mono channel with an explicit logical `channel` binding (the Signal stays mono forever; width lives at the graph boundary), so the logical output width derives to 3. Logical channels say nothing about hardware: `instruments/stereo-sub.io-map.json` is the matching device profile (`play instruments/stereo-sub.json --io-map instruments/stereo-sub.io-map.json`) routing the sub to a real device channel; without a profile the identity map applies, and a device with fewer channels drops the extra with a warning, never fatally. Play it: send /voicer/notes [midi, gate]. Tweak live: /sub/cutoff (sub crossover, Hz), /pan/pan (stereo position, -1..1).

nodes.doc: Equal-power stereo placement of the summed voices onto the mains.
Sub send: the pre-pan mono sum low-passed at 120 Hz onto its own channel.
